attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: sex_at_birth inferred male from the carer's use of 'he/his/him' throughout the chief complaint and HPI.
age_band child from stated age 7; paediatric_context populated and the no-paediatric-dosing rule applied.

Paediatric systemic antihistamine (cetirizine) dose withheld and flagged for in-person/clinician confirmation — the source-stated dose was not reproduced, per no-paediatric-dosing rule (protocol section 8). Topical terbinafine and hydrocortisone route+frequency retained as clinician-named fixed-concentration topicals (not weight-based systemic doses).

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician-measured vitals (BP/HR/RR/SpO2/temperature/weight), palpation/examination for induration, and saline cleansing of the excoriations were sealed to nodes 10/11 or reprojected into recognise + topical self-care + safety-net actions the carer can perform.

De-anchoring applied: the source note's confirmed diagnosis and cellulitis rule-out were removed from the presentation layer; 01/02 present only the carer's lay description before the diagnosis is known.
Provisional case_id SEQ 00042 derived from source DST-042 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.
geographic_context and detailed language/literacy not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

AMT/PBS candidate codes for terbinafine, hydrocortisone and cetirizine were not assigned (to avoid fabrication) and remain pending a terminology receipt; only the diagnosis SNOMED CT-AU + ICD-10-AM candidates are registered.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
